diagrams.title: ER Diagram Basic Symbols
diagrams.description: Shows the fundamental symbols used in ER diagrams: entities, attributes, relationships, and their visual representations. This diagram helps learners recognize and distinguish between the core components of ER notation.
diagrams.purpose: Introduces the standard notation symbols used to represent database concepts visually

diagrams.title: Entity with Attributes Example
diagrams.description: Demonstrates a complete entity (Student) with various attribute types: simple, composite, multivalued, and primary key. Shows how these are drawn and connected in ER notation.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how to represent different attribute types and their notation conventions

diagrams.title: Relationship Cardinality Notation
diagrams.description: Illustrates the three main cardinality types in ER diagrams: One-to-One (1:1), One-to-Many (1:N), and Many-to-Many (M:N). Shows how to visualize relationships between entities using these cardinalities.
diagrams.purpose: Teaches how to represent and interpret relationship cardinalities between entities
